c1.speaker: gary
c1.line: Prudence says she was here, night before the theft window closed. Cataloguing until three.

c2.speaker: narrator
c2.line: Evelyn sets down the shears — which counts, in this shop, as a formal oath.

c3.speaker: evelyn
c3.line: Until three in the morning, and I made the tea, and she read Jonah's half out loud in the voice she thinks is his. Write it down, Mr. Gibbons. Some alibis are also the best nights of a person's year.

c4.speaker: evelyn
c4.line: They weren't scandal, Mr. Gibbons. They were two people the town decided about before they'd decided about themselves.

c5.speaker: evelyn
c5.line: If the letter is ever found — let Prudence read it first. She's earned first.
